source: Edmunds, KBB, Consumer Reports, iSeeCars depreciation data
dataAgeNote: Car prices fluctuate. RICHY SHOULD WEB SEARCH for current used car prices. This data provides depreciation patterns and cost-of-ownership reference.

averageNewCar.note: Average new car loses 65% of its value in 5 years. A $35,000 car is worth ~$12,250 at year 5.

depreciation.bestValueAge: 3-5 years old. Someone else took the biggest depreciation hit but the car still has 60-70% of its useful life.

avgAnnualCosts.note: Average annual cost of owning a car beyond the purchase price: ~$5,600

evVsGas.note: EVs save ~$1,600/year in fuel and maintenance but typically cost $5,000-10,000 more upfront. Breakeven: 3-6 years.

buyingTips: Buy 3-5 year old cars for best value — 40-55% depreciation already absorbed
Toyota and Honda hold value best — you pay more upfront but lose less over time
Certified Pre-Owned (CPO) from dealers includes warranty but costs 5-10% more than private party
Private party sales are 10-20% cheaper than dealer but no warranty protection
Always get a pre-purchase inspection ($100-200) before buying used — saves thousands in hidden problems
Check vehicle history: Carfax ($39.99) or AutoCheck ($24.99). Non-negotiable.
Best months to buy: September-December (model year turnover + year-end dealer quotas)
Worst months: March-April (tax refund season = high demand)
newVsUsedCalculation: A $35,000 new car at 6% for 60 months = $676/month, $40,560 total. A 3-year-old version of the same car at $20,000 at 7% for 48 months = $479/month, $22,992 total. Savings: $197/month and $17,568 total.
